finding: claim_rotation_and_register INSERTs the successor refresh token without locking the users row, so under READ COMMITTED a concurrent end_session_atomically's `UPDATE refresh_tokens WHERE user_id=$1 AND revoked_at IS NULL` never scans the uncommitted successor. The successor survives the logout while the epoch moves to N+1; replaying it mints an access token stamped with the NEW epoch = a fully valid session. Logout defeated.

The SSE subscribe gate checks the epoch once, but the stream then lives until the token's exp (24h default); the 60s re-check only re-resolves is_active + profile::read, neither of which logout changes. An already-open stream survives logout. The client-side Session fan-out is not a boundary for a holder of a stolen token — they don't run our JS.
Same as the sync stream, but worse: the chat stream delivers live assistant CONTENT (not notify-only envelopes) for up to 24h past a logout.

refresh mapped ALL current_token_version errors to 401, including a transient DB error. The client treats a 401 from /auth/refresh as terminal (wipe + window.location.reload()), so a pool blip would log active users out mid-work and reload their page — a strictly larger blast radius than pre-diff. Inconsistent with the get_by_id mapping 30 lines above.

HOLLOW TEST: the spec billed as proving the SERVER-SIDE backstop used a same-context second tab, whose login form appears purely via shared-localStorage rehydration (tab 1's logout nulls the shared auth-storage token). It never issues a request bearing the revoked token and passes with the entire server-side feature reverted.

test_refresh_racing_a_logout_does_not_outlive_it asserts strictly less than its name/doc claim: it is fully sequential, so the read-before-claim ordering it purports to pin is irrelevant to whether it passes. The ordering had no executable coverage.

setAuthFromAutoLogin cleared hasPassword. Desktop's mid-session auto_login re-mint reaches it for the SAME identity with no follow-up initAuth(), so hasPassword flipped false and the profile page would offer 'set a password' until some later refetch. hasPassword is not a grant and not a leak vector.

tearDownSession's desktop contract relied solely on the caller checking refreshFallback, which desktop-base registers asynchronously. A terminal 401 during boot could slip past and reload the Tauri webview mid-startup.

The legacy jti-less refresh branch has no refresh_tokens row to revoke and re-mints via mint_session_tokens with the CURRENT epoch, so such a token survives logout. Pre-existing (revoke_all_for_user never covered it either) and effectively extinct, but the new doc comment overclaimed 'Ends EVERY session'.

DownloadTokenClaims is a separate signed credential (aud=ziee-download, 1h TTL) with no epoch, so a download token minted before logout keeps serving its file. Narrow: single file, owner-scoped, perms re-checked at download.

change-password and admin password-reset still only revoke refresh tokens (no epoch bump), so access tokens stay valid up to 24h after a password change. Pre-existing; explicitly descoped in DEC-8 (change-password needs a session re-mint in the response, which is real design work).

CLEAN: ver rides inside the signed JWT; Validation::default() pins HS256 and verifies the signature before Claims exist; no dangerous-decode path in the tree. Audience separation (ziee-api / -refresh / -download) blocks cross-type replay of a ver:None refresh token. The unwrap_or(0) allowance self-closes — any user who has logged out once is at >=1.
CLEAN: no deadlock. Logout locks users -> refresh_tokens; the FOR SHARE fix takes users FIRST in the claim path too, preserving that order.

CLEAN: the clearedSession refactor is correct at all four replaced sites. isInitializing:false is right at each; {...clearedSession, ...baseError} spread order preserves the originals' semantics exactly (baseError wins).

CLEAN: no reload loop. The wipe runs before reload(); zustand persist writes localStorage synchronously, so the reloaded tab rehydrates token:null and takes initAuth's early-out. Concurrent 401s collapse via refreshSessionInFlight + navigator.locks -> one reload.

CLEAN: ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ... NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 is metadata-only on PG11+ (no rewrite/long lock). Zero SELECT * FROM users, zero RETURNING *, every INSERT INTO users uses an explicit column list. 158 is the next free ordinal.

CLEAN: extractor coverage complete for request-scoped auth. All 6 FromRequestParts impls accounted for; JwtAuth+OptionalJwtAuth via assert_token_version_current, RequirePermissions+RequireAdmin via extract_authenticated_user -> verify_token_version; SyncOrigin/ConversationIdHeader are header parsers, not auth.

CLEAN: every access-token mint stamps a live epoch. Claims{} exists at exactly 4 sites; the 2 refresh-token constructions correctly carry ver:None; all 11 login-shaped flows funnel through mint_session_tokens; both generate_short_lived_jwt callers pass the current value.
